citation: gnomedic. (2024). LSHM: TTRPG Summer Break. Google Docs.
summary:
This short Low Stress High Mayhem handout supports a streamed session of gnomedic's *Summer Break*. It identifies the game, links to the Twitch VOD, points to gnomedic's social, Twitch, itch.io, and portfolio pages, and notes that the game appears in *The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book*, edited by James D'Amato. The document then gathers play-alikes, safety tools, GameRT links, Twitch chat norms, and a practical stream outline.

The play-alike section is organized around mood and audience rather than formal genre alone. It groups recommendations under "summer and nostalgia vibes," "kid at heart vibes," and "fort vibes," including games such as *Sleepaway*, *Camp Flying Moose for Girls of All Kinds*, *Two Summers*, *An Altogether Different River*, *Crescent Moon*, *BFF! Best Friends Forever*, *The Tragedies of Middle School*, *Kids on Bikes*, and *Fort*. This makes the handout a useful example of player advisory language for tabletop games: the recommendations connect theme, tone, life stage, and social feeling.

The document also embeds facilitation norms. It points to *Script Change*, *The Door Is Open*, and the TTRPG Safety Toolkit, then gives a Twitch policy emphasizing kindness, respect, no hate speech, moderator authority, harassment reporting, and private contact. The stream summary moves from tech check to introduction and rules, character creation, fort creation, danger creation, actual play, emotional check-in, and a post-play discussion about rules, playtesting, playing at different life stages, what childhood means to different people, programming potential, safety-tool considerations, and chat questions.

For the larger GBLS project, the item is useful as a practitioner artifact showing how an online game program can be scaffolded around theme, advisory context, safety, and reflection. Its strongest GBLS relevance is practical: it demonstrates how librarians or library-adjacent facilitators might frame a small TTRPG not just as entertainment, but as a structured event with discovery paths, moderation policy, emotional check-ins, and discussion prompts about memory, childhood, and life-stage interpretation.

This source should nevertheless remain outside the main literature review. It is not scholarship, not an evaluation, and not a sustained case study of library service. It may be useful later for an appendix on game advisory, stream facilitation, TTRPG safety resources, or low-stress online programming, but it should not be cited as evidence for GBLS outcomes.
sourceText:
What are we Playing?
Summer Break! by @gnomedic
Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A
Gnomedic’s Pages https://twitter.com/gnomedic?lang=en https://www.twitch.tv/gnomedic https://gnomedic.itch.io/ https://gnomedic.wixsite.com/portfolio
Found in: The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book edited by James D’amato https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/The-Ultimate-Micro-RPG-Book/James-D-Amato/TheUltimate-RPG-Guide-Series/9781507212868
James D’amato’s Pages https://twitter.com/OneShotRPG http://oneshotpodcast.com/
Play Alikes
Summer & Nostalgia Vibes ● Sleepaway : Possum Creek Games ● Camp Flying Moose For Girls of All Kinds: Alicia Furness ● Two Summers: Côme Martin ● An Altogether Different River: Aaron Lim
Kid at Heart Vibes ● Crescent Moon: Pocket Compass Co. ● BFF! Best Friends Forever: Heart of the Deernicorn ● The Tragedies of Middle School: 9th Level Games ● Kids on Bikes: Renegade Game Studios
Fort Vibes ● Fort: Leder Games
Extra Resources ● Safety Tools ○ Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ○ The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ■ Blog by Lizzie Stark ■ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk

■ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ■ Google Drive
Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
Stream Summary
Tech Check on Twitch then Go Time!
Part 1: Intro and Rules ● Who are we & what are we doing??? ● Breakdown of Stream ● Breakdown of Game ○ Character Creation ○ Fort Creation ○ Danger Creation ● Segue: Ready to play?
Part 2: Actual Play ● Fun Stuff ● Segue: How do you Feel?
Part 3: Discussion ● Figuring out Rules & Playtesting ● Playing at different life stages ○ What childhood means to different people ● Programing Potential ● Safety Tools Considerations ● Chat Questions & Comments ● Segue: That’s all we’ve got for this session…

Ending ● Come back again on September 9th for our newest twitch program the Round Table round table, where we’ve gathered our nerdiest librarians to talk about all things games ● Thank you so much for joinings for ● Russell: Low Stress and ● Danielle: High Mayhem ● Bye!!

Intended_Outcome.not_applicable.quote: Extra Resources ● Safety Tools ○ Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ○ The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ■ Blog by Lizzie Stark ■ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk
Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3.

Part 3: Discussion ● Figuring out Rules & Playtesting ● Playing at different life stages ○ What childhood means to different people ● Programing Potential ● Safety Tools Considerations ● Chat Questions & Comments ● Segue: That’s all we’ve got for this session…
